rulings.Case Title: JIALIANG DONG VS. UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO OF LAW
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, October 3, 2025, line 1, PETITIONER JIALIANG DONG , AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Hearing required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: MUSEUM PLAZA LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL VS. BERDING & WEIL LLP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, October 3, 2025, line 2, 3 - CROSS DEFENDANT KATALYST DEVELOPMENT MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Cross-Defendant Katalyst Development, LLC's motion for summary adjudication is denied. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, October 3, 2025, line 5, 2 - PLAINTIFF ROSA MARTINEZ , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF SEBASTIAN MEJIA. Motion To Compel Airbnb Defendants' Responses To Request For Production Of Documents The court concludes that Airbnb is in substantial compliance with its discovery obligations with respect to the RFPs and is entitled to relief from waiver. rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: SANCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, October 3, 2025, line 6, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: SANCTIONS On September 9, 2025, the court issued an order to American Honda Motor Co., Inc. to show cause why it should not be sanctioned $1,635, payable to plaintiff Adelemo Cruz, for failure to carry out its obligations under the parties' settlement agreement. rulings.Case Title: KURT GRIMES VS. RUSHMORE LOAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: KURT GRIMES NOTICE OF MOTION TO DEEM ADMISSIONS ADMITTED
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, October 3, 2025, line 13, 1 - KURT GRIMES' NOTICE OF MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS Plaintiff's motion to deem matters admitted is denied. The Moorer Declaration establishes that defendants' RFA responses were served by mail on August 19, 2025. This was timely service even if they responses did not arrive to Plaintiff until several days later. Service is deemed complete at the time the document is deposited in the mail. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1013, subd. (a).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KURT GRIMES VS. RUSHMORE LOAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: KURT GRIMES NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL INITIAL RESPONSES
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, October 3, 2025, line 13 2 - KURT GRIMES' MOTION TO COMPEL INITIAL RESPONSES Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to his form interrogatories, set one, and requests for production, set one, is denied. The Moorer Declaration establishes that defendants' discovery responses were served by mail on August 19, 2025. This was timely service even if they responses did not arrive to Plaintiff until several days later. Service is deemed complete at the time the document is deposited in the mail. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1013, subd. (a).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.
